summary: Publish a public-safe Family Bertus case study
description: Add Family Bertus to the Apps hub as a private deployment and publish a first-party case-study page that explains its WhatsApp-first family operations model without exposing names, messages, identifiers, credentials, or private infrastructure.
acceptance: Apps exposes Family Bertus as a private deployment linking to /family-bertus/.
The case study explains the family-native interaction model, useful automations, human confirmation boundaries, and privacy boundary in visitor-facing English.
No real names, messages, chat identifiers, tokens, endpoints, or operational secrets are published.
Canonical URL, sitemap, analytics, favicons, responsive layout, and accessibility match the current site contract.

evidence.summary: npm run check:ci passed: 56 tests, 142 public pages, 0 SEO regressions; desktop browser QA passed with zero page overflow and clean current-site styling; 390px mobile screenshots passed for the case-study flow; public-safe scan found no credential or routing markers; timeline and sitemap connected.
